reviews.review_msg:
She genuinely cares about her students, but reconsider it if you have a heavy course load. Weekly chapter and case readings often exceed 60 pages, plus two-page weekly case essays. 

Throughout the term, about 80% of grades were not released, so you won't be able to know how you did on your group projects. Final grade took a long time to be posted

I don't usually rate my professors but Natalie is by far my favourite professor at SFU!! This is my second time taking a course with her and she is the absolute best. She is the most caring, approachable and sweetest human ever. I HIGHLY RECOMMEND
Natalie is such a caring and great prof! Her classes aren't heavy on exams and she puts a focus on projects/participation instead! Definitely recommend her class if you get the chance to take it!

I enjoyed this class. Very little of the class was spent just listening to lecture. Lots of discussions and activities. Groups are formed and they present a chapter and case study 1 week in the semester. After each presentation Natalie ensures all important concepts are covered. Natalie is friendly, good at explaining concepts. Highly recommend.
The kindest prof I have ever had!

Natalie is one of the best profs I have had a SFU. She is kind, willing to help and engage in discussions to help your group with the projects, and her lectures are never boring. She tries to give examples from her experience that are relevant to the weeks material. The course content is 100% useful for a career in HR. I am taking her other class.

Anyone who gives Natalie less than 5/5 is lying. She is hands down one of the best professors I have ever had. She is a fair marker and provides a ton of feedback on assignments and presentations. She is always accessible to answer questions and genuinely wants her students to succeed. Do yourself a favour and take this course with her.

Exceptional professor in SFU. She really shows her care to the class. Natalie is the professor that will always go above and beyond than just read the book off and give grades, she will instead bring up useful materials and news in current HR world and let the students be updated. If you are in HR, make sure you have a class with her!

One of the upper division HR courses where participation is like 30% of your grade. Read the chapter/case and come to class prepared. Sounds boring, but, Natalie makes this course engaging and fun. She cares about her students and gives great feedback even if you weren't asking for it. Overall a really nice prof and would definitely recommend her.

Great prof and lectures are always interesting. Get ready to read and discuss case studies every week! Nearing the end of the semester, you will be preparing recommendations to help a real company mitigate/solve its issues.
